title: After-Action Report — Cricket Batting Order: the optional rule (scaffold + first flip)
scope: This AAR covers the scope decision and the optional-rule scaffold — the gate, the per-league plumbing, and the first (automatic) flip mechanic. A follow-on AAR turns the automatic flip into a manager decision; this document is the historical record of the foundation.

rationale.item: Cricket's defining order rule — bat in fixed order, retired once dismissed, all-out ends the innings — conflicts head-on with O27's 27-out half (a ~9–12 batter side would be all-out long before 27 outs). So I clarified scope up front and landed on a much smaller, native-feeling mechanic, shipped as an optional, per-league rule (the Power Play pattern).

The existing cycle machinery already had the needed signal: Team.advance_lineup detects the wrap, increments lineup_cycle_number, and resets jokers_used_this_cycle — exactly the record of "was a joker deployed this trip?", readable at the wrap before it's cleared.

delegation_events.decision: Reorder-only mechanic, shipped as an optional per-league rule
delegation_events.quote: The user chose **reorder-only**, shipped as an **optional per-league rule**.

delegation_events.decision: Two scoping questions surfaced before building (meaning of the rule; global vs per-league)
delegation_events.quote: I surfaced the conflict with the 27-out core and asked: (1) what should "cricket batting order" mean — full dismissal model, partnerships, reorder-only, or a design doc; and (2) how broad — global change or optional per-league rule.

actions.description: Gate: cricket_order_on(team) — per-team override first, else global config default (on if league opted in OR global default on)
Global default cfg.CRICKET_BATTING_ORDER_ENABLED, auto-exposed on the Engine Settings dashboard via engine_config
Storage: teams.cricket_order_enabled column (CREATE TABLE + idempotent ALTER migration)
Per-game read: sim.py stamps the override on both teams when the league opted in
UI opt-in: new-league checkbox, peer-universe per-league select, /league/edit toggle for existing leagues, with app.py handlers
First (automatic) flip mechanic inside advance_lineup: on a joker-free wrap, reverse the lineup for the next cycle (later superseded by the manager decision)

risk_posture: So no other rule (outs, stays, jokers, walk-back, Declared Seconds) changes; the rule only reorders the existing cycle, and only when a league turns it on.
Mirrors Power Play end to end, so the two optional rules behave identically with respect to global-vs-per-league control

validation_claims.claim: New engine test module exercising the flip and the gate; full o27/tests suite green
Off-by-default verified inert; on-by-default flips fire in live games (verified: 8 flips/game with two joker-less sides; the #9-hitting pitcher led off the next cycle)
validation_claims.check_method: Run live games with rule off and on, inspect lineup order

validation_claims.claim: DB column + migration verified on fresh and legacy teams tables; all changed Python byte-compiles; edited Jinja templates parse

non_actions.item: The flip as a manager decision (earn / use-or-lose / persona / regulation only)
No new stats; no dismissal/all-out model
non_actions.reason: out of scope — breaks the 27-out half

residual_risks: The automatic flip had a rendering gap (the raw apply_event log is discarded when a Renderer is present) — fixed by the follow-on event-based redesign
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — Cricket Batting Order: the optional rule (scaffold + first flip)
unmapped_content: Header metadata: Branch claude/o27-cricket-batting-order-k6hhp; followed by the manager-decision and flip-aware-lineups AARs; living spec docs/feature-cricket-batting-order.md. Rule statement: when enabled, the batting order flips 1-9 → 9-1 at the end of a trip through the order that the side completed without deploying a joker; using a joker locks the order for that cycle.
parser_notes: Date from 'Date completed:' line. Three same-day AARs on one branch form a sequence.
